mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation by lowering discount rates and supporting risk appetite, so S&P 500 forward returns are positive when policy stays near the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above recession-like levels signals ongoing earnings pressure and weak household income, so S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next month when UNRATE remains elevated or continues rising.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average forward return is not below unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rebound in housing starts marks an early recovery in cyclical demand and construction activity, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by more than 8% from its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts expansions are followed by negative or flat 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is improving while payroll employment is also rising, the economy is likely moving from contraction toward expansion, so the joint signal should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone in walk-forward tests, or if triggered periods do not show higher 20-day returns than unconditional by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in retail sales captures weakening consumer demand that feeds directly into revenue expectations for broad index constituents, so a month-over-month decline in RSAFS should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls below its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales declines are not followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if the average 5-day return is non-negative in triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid declines in CPI inflation while the policy rate remains near zero indicate disinflation without immediate tightening pressure, which should support equity multiples and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change falls by more than 1 percentage point and FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes with low FEDFUNDS do not yield positive 20-day returns above baseline, or if the joint signal's IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained payrolls recovery during a high-unemployment regime indicates that labor-market stress is easing before broad earnings recovery is visible, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains above 8% while PAYEMS rises for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if this high-unemployment recovery pattern fails to produce positive 60-day returns in a majority of cases, or if returns are lower than when PAYEMS rises without high UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in real GDP after contraction reflects broad demand stabilization and improves earnings visibility, so quarterly output recovery should align with positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises sequentially after a prior quarterly decline
failure_condition: Reject if sequential GDP recoveries are not associated with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than baseline, or if the conditional mean return is not positive.
